data.title: 'Silver Prices Face Turbulence': Goldman Sachs Sees More Wild Swings Ahead
data.description:
Silver (SLV) prices are holding firm above the psychologically important $100-per-ounce mark today, with spot levels hovering in the low-$100s as traders digest another leg of the metal's remarkable run. The move caps off an extraordinary period for silver, which surged 138% last year and has remained elevated in recent sessions.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Part of what makes silver's rally so dramatic is not just demand, but the way the market is structured. In a recent research note, Goldman Sachs' commodities strategists argued that silver's extreme price swings are being amplified by unusually tight inventories in key trading hubs, particularly London, where much of the world's benchmark pricing is set.

"Extreme price action is likely to persist," the bank warned, pointing to thin available supply and reduced market liquidity as key drivers of volatility.

Goldman explained that concerns over potential trade restrictions and tariffs last year triggered a rush to move physical silver into the United States, draining stocks from London vaults and distorting the global flow of metal. That shift has made prices far more sensitive to incremental buying and selling. According to the bank, levels of weekly demand that would historically move prices by about 2% are now capable of driving swings closer to 7%, simply because there is less readily available metal to absorb those flows.

Crucially, Goldman does not see the rally as proof of a fundamental shortage of silver. Instead, the strategists describe the market as suffering from a "location problem" rather than a production problem – silver exists, but not always where the trading system needs it most. Until inventories are rebuilt and liquidity improves, the bank believes sharp rallies and pullbacks are likely to remain a defining feature of the market.

Recent developments appear to support that view. Ongoing geopolitical tensions have kept safe-haven demand alive, while expectations for lower U.S. interest rates have increased the appeal of precious metals more broadly. At the same time, policy changes affecting metal exports and physical supply chains continue to inject uncertainty into the market, reinforcing the conditions Goldman says can exaggerate price moves.

Is Silver Worth Buying Right Now?

A look at TipRanks' technical analysis dashboard suggests silver is tilting bullish overall. In the one-day summary, 15 indicators point to a Buy signal, compared with just one Neutral and six Bearish readings. The strength is coming primarily from moving averages, which flash a Strong Buy with 12 Bullish signals and none Bearish, even as oscillators lean the other way with a Sell reading.
data.author: Ben Yoffe

data.title: Why Investors May Get Paid Sooner from Autonomous Cars than Humanoid Robots
data.description:
Autonomous vehicles have a clearer near-term investing edge than humanoid robots, according to tech venture capitalist Bill Gurley. Speaking on Yahoo Finance's Opening Bid Unfiltered, Gurley said that self-driving cars are further along because the problems they solve are more predictable and defined. In contrast, humanoid robots are still in an early phase, where the industry has not yet agreed on what they should be best used for or how they should work at scale.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Gurley also explained that most humanoid robot projects today are highly customized for specific warehouses or environments, making it hard to create a single standard product that works well everywhere. As a result, scaling humanoid robots into a large, profitable business is much more difficult right now. It's worth noting that Gurley's views carry weight given his long career, which includes early investments in Uber (UBER), Twitter (now X), and Nextdoor (NXDR).

Still, Gurley emphasized that both autonomous cars and humanoid robots are challenging businesses because they require heavy hardware investment and large amounts of capital. Nevertheless, this hasn't stopped Tesla (TSLA) CEO Elon Musk from pursuing both opportunities at the same time, although Musk has also admitted that early production will be slow. In addition, a recent Gartner report found that by 2028, fewer than 20 companies are expected to move humanoid robots into full production. This shows just how early the technology is.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 10 Buys, eight Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $398.38 per share implies 8.5% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Nvidia (NVDA) to Sign Memory Chip Deal with Samsung
data.description:
Multiple media reports say that Nvidia (NVDA) is planning to source memory chips from Samsung Electronics (SSNLF).

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Under terms of the deal, Samsung will supply Nvidia with next-generation memory chips. Samsung is reportedly ready to begin producing high-bandwidth memory chips, called "HBM4," as early as February of this year and start shipping them to Nvidia.

Samsung is a global leader when it comes to making HBM memory microchips, which are critically important for the latest artificial-intelligence (AI) processors. Surging demand for HBM chips has been a big driver of Samsung's sales in recent quarters.

Impacts on Micron Stock (MU)

News of Samsung's contract win has shares of rival Micron Technology (MU) down about 3%. Micron is the chief rival of Samsung when it comes to the memory chip market. However, Samsung's progress in meeting requirements for Nvidia doesn't mean a loss of sales for Micron, with mega-cap technology companies racing to buy as many HBM memory chips as they can.

In fact, Micron's supply of HBM memory chips for 2026 is sold out. Micron CEO Sanjay Mehrotra told analysts on the company's most recent earnings call that the company is ramping up its HBM memory chip production to meet future demand.

Is NVDA Stock a Buy?

Nvidia's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 42 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 40 Buy, one Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the past three months. The average NVDA price target of $263.11 implies 41.10% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: The Wall Street Slayer on Nvidia (NVDA), CoreWeave (CRWV), and the Silicon Ouroboros of AI Hype
data.description:
There's something almost terrifying about how the AI boom has turned circular logic into a multi-trillion-dollar circus. Take CoreWeave (CRWV). On Monday, shares of the crypto-bros-turned-AI-hustlers jumped 6% after Nvidia (NVDA) announced it was throwing another $2 billion at the company. The "investment" is supposed to help build over 5 gigawatts of AI "factory" capacity by 2030.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Investors applauded like they'd just witnessed Elon landing on Mars without the ability to get back to Earth rather than noticing the obvious: a supplier is funding a customer so the customer can buy more from the supplier. This, investors, is what passes for momentum these days.

Nvidia sells the chips. Nvidia funds the infrastructure company that buys the chips. The infrastructure company borrows against the chips to buy more chips. The market rewards everyone involved for "execution." Somewhere in the middle of this loop, someone drops the phrase "AI native software," Wall Street applauds as if it's Pulitzer-worthy, and the share price levitates another few percent.

At the center, of course, is Jensen Huang, Nvidia's leather-jacketed high priest, giving keynote speeches like Moses descending from Mount CUDA. Every sentence drips destiny: AI factories. New industrial revolutions. Gazillion-dollar opportunities. And yes, Nvidia has built something remarkable. But right now it feels less like innovation and more like Huang quietly realizing the best customer for Nvidia… is Nvidia itself.

And then there's CoreWeave, now hailed as a pillar of next-generation AI infrastructure, despite starting life as a crypto miner called Atlantic Crypto. Really. When Bitcoin stopped paying for Lambos, they pivoted to renting GPUs to anyone willing to say "large language model" without laughing. Although with the Trumps now in crypto – American Bitcoin, Melania coin, yes we made it cypherpunks! – the grift just went up a notch or a thousand and this pivot feels less like foresight and more like self-preservation. If the original crypto miners are running to less corrupt corners, can you really blame them? Analysts applaud this as visionary adaptability, but it's the same reckless greed in a slightly smarter suit.

Look, The Slayer isn't saying AI demand is fake. There is real demand. It's that so much growth is being propped up by companies investing in each other to keep the story tidy. Nvidia owns a chunk of CoreWeave. CoreWeave depends on Nvidia hardware. Both profit when the market pretends this counts as organic expansion rather than balance-sheet gymnastics.

And the market doesn't just tolerate it – it salivates over it. Circular deals keep the wheel spinning while giving everyone the illusion of control. They avoid awkward questions about energy costs, utilization rates, or what happens when "build it and they will come" turns into "we built it and now we're funding ourselves to pretend they arrived."

So, the snake keeps eating its tail and the valuations edge higher. But somewhere in the background, reality is waiting, like it always does, for the music to stop.
data.author: Wall Street Slayer

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Climb Despite Surging Shutdown Odds and Trump's Canada Tariff Threat
data.description:
In a show of strength, both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed higher on Monday, shrugging off rising odds of a government shutdown and President Trump's potential tariffs on Canada.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Senate Democrats have vowed to vote against a government funding bill that includes appropriations for the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) after 37-year-old Alex Pretti was fatally shot by a federal agent in Minneapolis. A total of 12 bills are needed to fully fund the government, with six already approved. The remaining six have been grouped into one package, with Democrats proposing that funding for DHS be voted on separately in order to avoid a shutdown.

"Take up DHS by itself, let's have an honest negotiation, put some guardrails on what's going on, some accountability, and that would solve this problem," said Senator Angus King of Maine. The odds of a government shutdown have ripped higher to 81% from 10% on Friday on prediction platform Polymarket.

Minnesota Governor Tim Walz noted that Trump is considering reducing the number of federal agents in the state after the pair spoke over the phone on Monday morning. That comes after Trump announced he would send "Border Czar" and Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) veteran Tom Homan to assist with the crackdown in Minnesota.

On the tariff front, Trump threatened a 100% tariff on Canadian imports if the country signs a trade deal with China that involves lowering duties on the first 49,000 Chinese electric vehicle imports to 6.1% from 100%. He warned in a Truth Social post that China would "eat Canada alive, completely devour it."

It wasn't all bad news, as U.S. durable goods orders, which track demand for goods designed to last at least three years, signaled that companies may be looking past tariffs. New orders for November jumped 5.3% month-over-month, above the consensus estimate of 3.8%. The growth was driven by a strong showing in non-defense aircraft, with orders jumping by 97.6%. During the month, Boeing (BA) reported 164 orders, up from 15 in October. In addition, core durable goods orders, which exclude transportation, ticked higher by 0.5% and surpassed the estimate of 0.3%.

Looking ahead, the Fed will hold its first Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting of the year on January 28, where it is widely expected to hold rates steady amid signs of stabilization within the labor market.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.50% gain, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) returned 0.42%.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: SoFi Stock (SOFI): Bulls and Bears Weigh In Ahead of Q4 Earnings
data.description:
SoFi Technologies (SOFI), a digital financial services and fintech company, is set to report its fourth-quarter 2025 earnings on Friday, January 30.  The stock has rallied about 60.7% in 2025, easily outperforming the broader market. After a strong run over the past year, investors are split on what comes next. Some see steady growth and improving profits, while others remain cautious about valuation and execution risks. Here's a clear look at what bulls and bears are saying ahead of earnings.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Bull Case for SoFi

1. Revenue Growth and Improving Profits: Bulls point to SoFi's steady revenue growth and improving profitability. In the last reported third quarter, the company's revenue grew 38% year-over-year and reached a new quarterly record of $950 million. Also, adjusted earnings came in at 11 cents per share, topping analysts' consensus estimate of 8 cents. Growth was driven by higher loan originations, stronger fee income, and rising deposits. Bulls argue this shows SoFi's business model is starting to scale.

2. Expanding Member Base: SoFi has continued to add new members at a healthy pace. Total members and products both grew sharply year over year, reflecting demand across lending, banking, and investing. It added 905,000 new members in Q3, bringing its total to 12.6 million, up 35% from last year. It also added 1.4 million new products, reaching 18.6 million in total. Bulls see this as a key long-term strength, as more members give SoFi room to cross-sell products and grow revenue per user over time.

3. Analysts Still See Upside: Several Wall Street analysts remain positive on SoFi ahead of earnings, pointing to management's improving execution. They also note that easing interest-rate pressure could support loan demand and margins in 2026. Even after a strong run in 2025, SoFi's average price target still suggests about 9% upside from current levels.

The Bear Case for SOFI

1. Valuation Still a Concern: Despite recent gains, bears say SoFi looks expensive compared with traditional banks and many fintech peers. The stock trades at a forward price-to-earnings ratio of about 71, far above the sector average near 11, showing investors are paying a high premium. With strong growth already priced in, any slowdown in revenue, loan growth, or margins could pressure the stock after earnings.

2. Credit and Loan Risks: Skeptics remain cautious about credit quality, especially if economic conditions weaken. While SoFi has stressed its focus on higher-quality borrowers, bears say rising delinquencies or higher loan losses could weigh on results and investor confidence.

3. Earnings Expectations Are High: With SoFi now expected to deliver consistent profits, the bar has been raised. Bears argue that simply meeting expectations may not be enough. Investors will be closely watching guidance for 2026, and any cautious tone from management could lead to short-term volatility.

What to Expect on January 30

Wall Street expects SoFi to report earnings of $0.12 per share, down 58.6% from the same period last year. Meanwhile, revenue is projected to come in at $977.42 million, compared with $1.008 billion in the prior-year quarter.

For investors confident in SoFi's long-term story, the focus will be on loan demand, credit quality, and guidance beyond this quarter. More cautious investors may choose to stay patient, waiting for clearer signals on profitability and management's outlook before taking a position. 

What Is the Price Target for SOFI? 

Overall, Wall Street is sidelined on SoFi stock with a Hold consensus rating based on five Buys, six Holds, and three Sell recommendations. The average SOFI stock price target of $28.31 indicates a possible upside of 8.34% from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Nike (NKE) to Cut 775 Jobs as Part of Automation Push
data.description:
Nike (NKE) is cutting 775 employees as the sneaker giant accelerates its use of manufacturing automation.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

These are the latest layoffs by Beaverton, Oregon-based Nike as it tries to boost its bottom line and share price. Last summer, the sports apparel maker announced 1,000 corporate job cuts. The new workforce reduction will mostly impact workers at Nike's distribution centers in Tennessee and Mississippi.

In a written statement sent to media, Nike said the latest layoffs are aimed at reducing "complexity, improve flexibility, and build a more responsive, resilient, responsible, and efficient operation." The company has repeatedly said that it plans to create a leaner workforce as part of its goal to get back to "long-term, profitable growth."

Nike's Turnaround Strategy

The layoffs come as Nike CEO Elliott Hill works to turn around the company after years of slowing sales and shrinking profit margins. The 775 job cuts also come as artificial intelligence and automation sweep across corporate America, impacting distribution centers and their workers. Last year, delivery giant UPS (UPS) announced plans to cut 48,000 roles as it adopts more automation at its warehouses.

"We're taking steps to strengthen and streamline our operations so we can move faster, operate with greater discipline, and better serve athletes and consumers," Nike said in the statement. "We are sharpening our supply chain footprint, accelerating the use of advanced technology and automation, and investing in the skills our teams need for the future."

The struggles came after its former top executive John Donahoe pursued a direct selling strategy that prioritized the retailer's stores and websites over wholesale partners.

As part of that strategy, Nike's distribution centers – and staff within those facilities – ballooned, but they don't have the volume to support those staffing levels, the people familiar with the matter said.

Under Hill, Nike has been working to woo back wholesale partners, clean out stale inventory and reignite innovation. When reporting fiscal second quarter earnings in December, Nike said its net income had fallen 32% as it contends with tariffs, costs associated with its turnaround and a slowdown in its key China market.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: 2026 F-Line E Gives Ford Stock (NYSE:F) Electric Semi Ambitions, No Help in Share Prices
data.description:
We all knew that legacy automaker Ford (F) had ambitions of getting into the electric vehicle market. It even realized these ambitions to some extent, before paring them down in the face of market reality. But some of those ambitions made a comeback, as Ford got in on the electric semi stakes with its new F-Line E truck. This did not bring much comfort to investors, though, who sent shares down fractionally in Monday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The F-Line E will hit roads in Western Europe this summer, reports note, and be available in a choice of four-by-two or six-by-two configurations. The F-Line E is rated for up to 26 tons, and will be powered by a set of four modular batteries. The batteries are nickel manganese cobalt (NMC) packs that offer around 98 kWh each. This gives the F-Line E a range of about 300 kilometers, or roughly, 185 miles.

Perhaps the best part here is charging speed. The batteries on the F-Line E can be recharged from 20% to 80% in about 45 minutes, which is about the time it takes to unload the truck. Plugging the truck in when unloading starts will have the truck mostly ready to turn around by the time it is emptied, reports note. If the truck needs to be reloaded, the chances the batteries will fully charge in that time only improve.

The Blue Light Special

K-Mart is pretty much a dead brand these days, ever since it was purchased by Sears some time ago. Some K-Mart locations remain, yes, but most of these are not even in the United States. This was a bit of a problem for a car service tech, who recently opened a car's hood to find a K-1 oil filter in place. This would not be such a problem, except K-1 oil filters were a K-Mart product.

The filter was found on a Ford vehicle, the engine compartment of which was found to be coated in a "…mass of black, caked-on gunk." But given that the engine in question was a 1964 Y-block that came out of a 1964 F-100 before being installed into a 1954 F-100, it is hard to blame the oil filter fully for failing here. Not even the service tech blames the filter; he noted that engine conditions of the era often meant sludge was a normal by-product.

Is Ford Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on F stock based on two Buys, 11 Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 30.64% rally in its share price over the past year, the average F price target of $13.87 per share implies 2.82% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: 'Worth a Shot, But…' Says Investor on Amazon Stock
data.description:
Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) wasn't one of the market's big AI winners last year, even as the AI revolution kicked into high gear and propelled many of the sector's heavyweights to new highs. Instead, the stock lagged the broader market, with gains of just 2% over the past year.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ironically enough, the company's recent performance has been quite strong. Amazon generated $180.2 billion in revenue in Q3, up 12% year over year, while AWS posted a 20.2% increase in sales – its fastest growth in nearly three years. Meanwhile, the cloud provider reported a backlog of $200 billion, pointing to a sizable pipeline of revenue still to come.

Several factors weighed on the stock in 2025, including investor unease over heavy capital spending, a valuation that began the year near 40 times earnings, and broader concerns about the economic outlook.

So, could this be the year that AMZN's share price catches up to some of its hyperscaler peers? Investor Vladimir Dimitrov – and long-time AMZN bear – is changing his tune on the company.

"Amazon's underwhelming stock performance in recent years could change course in 2026," the 5-star investor opined.

While Dimitrov acknowledges the concerns about the circular AI financing deals sloshing around, the investor notes that the base case scenario in 2026 points to "robust" demand for cloud infrastructure and services. AWS's improving profitability over the past few years gives Dimitrov plenty of confidence that the company can succeed in taking advantage of this ongoing trend.

Still, the investor offers a major caveat or two for why an investment in Amazon may not pay off.

"The big unknown for AMZN share price in 2026 now remains the state of the economy and the equity market overall," adds Dimitrov.

Amazon isn't just a cloud provider, and its e-commerce segment could suffer if consumer confidence levels plummet in the year ahead, according to Dimitrov. Moreover, AMZN could be in bigger trouble than other equities if things go south, as it tends to be more volatile than the overall market.

"Either of these scenarios would most likely mean yet another year of underperformance for Amazon," the investor concludes.

All that being said, Dimitrov is willing to take the chance, rating AMZN a Buy. (To watch Vladimir Dimitrov's track record, click here)

Wall Street, meanwhile, is clearly optimistic. With 46 Buys and just 1 Hold, Amazon earns a Strong Buy consensus rating. The stock's average 12-month price target of $295.05 implies upside of 23% over the next 12 months. (See AMZN stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: ChatGPT and Claude Begin Referencing Elon Musk's Grokipedia in Some Responses
data.description:
Some responses generated by OpenAI's ChatGPT have recently included references to Grokipedia, an AI-generated encyclopedia created by Elon Musk's rival AI firm, xAI. However, these citations appeared only in a limited number of user queries, rather than broadly across the platform. Nevertheless, this behavior first came to light due to reporting by The Guardian, which noted that the references were unusual given that Grokipedia is a new and experimental source compared with more established knowledge bases. For context, Grokipedia launched in October as part of xAI's attempt to build an alternative to Wikipedia.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Unlike Wikipedia, which relies on human editors and moderators, Grokipedia is entirely AI-generated. In theory, this allows it to avoid human bias, though that assumption remains debated. It has also allowed the platform to grow extremely fast. In fact, Grokipedia has already produced 5,615,201 articles, which is nearly 79% of the size of English Wikipedia's 7,119,376 entries. In addition, The Guardian found that ChatGPT cited Grokipedia nine times while answering more than a dozen test questions.

Interestingly, though, these citations did not appear for well-known or widely documented topics, but instead showed up when users asked about more obscure historical or biographical claims. That pattern suggests selective use rather than a heavy reliance. The publication also noted that Anthropic's AI assistant Claude showed similar behavior. In response, an OpenAI spokesperson told The Guardian that ChatGPT draws from a wide range of sources while applying safety filters and clearly displaying citations. Meanwhile, Anthropic declined to comment, while xAI responded by saying, "Legacy media lies."

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

When it comes to Elon Musk's companies, most of them are privately held. However, retail investors can invest in his most popular company, Tesla (TSLA). Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 10 Buys, eight Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $398.38 per share implies 8.8% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri
